NCT01626079.name: COAPT

allOutcomes.title: HF hospitalization or ACM at 24 months (composite)

NCT01626079.snippet: Source: Stone GW et al. NEJM 2018;379:2307-2318.

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.source: Stone GW et al. NEJM 2018;379:2307-2318, CONSORT
evidence.text: 614 patients with HFrEF (LVEF 20-50%) and moderate-severe (3+) or severe (4+) secondary MR despite maximal guideline-directed medical therapy were randomized to MitraClip + GDMT (n=302) or GDMT alone (n=312).

evidence.label: Primary Outcome — All HF Hospitalizations at 24 months
evidence.source: Stone GW et al. NEJM 2018;379:2307-2318, Table 2
evidence.text: Annualized rate of HF hospitalizations at 24 months: 35.8% per patient-year with MitraClip vs 67.9% with GDMT alone (HR 0.53; 95% CI 0.40-0.70; P<0.001). Composite primary + all-cause mortality HR 0.57 (0.45-0.71).

evidence.label: All-Cause Mortality at 24 months
evidence.source: Stone GW et al. NEJM 2018;379:2307-2318, Table 2
evidence.text: ACM at 24 months: 29.1% (MitraClip) vs 46.1% (GDMT); HR 0.62 (95% CI 0.46-0.82; P<0.001).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 Randomization LOW — 1:1 stratified central randomization. D2 Deviations SOME CONCERNS — open-label procedure, but adjudicated events. D3 Missing Data LOW — >97% follow-up. D4 Outcome LOW — independent blinded CEC. D5 Selective Reporting LOW — pre-specified primary with SAP lock.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~1. Symptomatic functional MR (≥3+) due to cardiomyopathy of either ischemic or non-ischemic etiology determined by assessment of a qualifying transthoracic echocardiogram (TTE) obtained within 90 days and transesophageal echocardiogram (TEE) obtained within 180 days prior to subject registration, with MR severity based principally on the TTE study, confirmed by the Echocardiography Core Lab (ECL). The ECL may request a transesophageal echocardiogram (TEE) to confirm MR etiol...

NCT01920698.name: MITRA-FR

allOutcomes.title: ACM or unplanned HF hospitalization at 12 months (composite)

NCT01920698.snippet: Source: Obadia JF et al. NEJM 2018;379:2297-2306 (MITRA-FR).

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.source: Obadia JF et al. NEJM 2018;379:2297-2306, CONSORT
evidence.text: 307 French patients with severe functional MR (EROA >20 mm^2 or regurgitant volume >30 mL) and LVEF 15-40% were randomized to percutaneous MitraClip + medical therapy (n=152) or medical therapy alone (n=155).

evidence.label: Primary Outcome — Death or HF Hospitalization at 12 months
evidence.text: Composite primary: 83/152 (54.6%) MitraClip vs 78/155 (51.3%) control (OR 1.16; 95% CI 0.73-1.84; HR 1.13, 0.86-1.47). Result NEUTRAL — inconsistent with COAPT.

evidence.label: Population Differences vs COAPT
evidence.source: Pibarot et al. JACC Cardiovasc Imaging 2019;12:353
evidence.text: MITRA-FR patients had less severe MR (mean EROA 31 mm^2 vs 41 mm^2 in COAPT), larger LV (mean LVEDV 250 mL vs 192 mL), and lower baseline LVEF. This population-enrichment hypothesis partly explains divergent trial outcomes.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Age > 18 years old~* Severe secondary mitral regurgitation characterised, according to the European guidelines and recommendations, by a regurgitant volume>30 mL/beat or an effective regurgitant orifice>20 mm².~* New York heart Association Class≥ II.~* Left ventricular ejection fraction between 15% and 40%~* Minimum of 1 hospitalization for heart failure within 12 months preceding randomization~* Optimal standard of care therapy for heart failure according to investigat...

NCT02444338.name: RESHAPE-HF2

allOutcomes.title: Recurrent HF hospitalization or CV death at 24 months (composite)

NCT02444338.snippet: Source: Anker SD et al. NEJM 2024 (RESHAPE-HF2, Goettingen-sponsored international RCT).

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.text: 505 patients with symptomatic HFrEF/HFmrEF (LVEF 20-50%, NYHA II-IV) and moderate-to-severe or severe secondary MR despite optimal GDMT were randomized 1:1 to MitraClip + GDMT (n=255) or GDMT alone (n=250).

evidence.label: Primary Composite — Composite of CV death or recurrent HF hospitalization at 24 months
evidence.text: Rate of recurrent HF hospitalizations or CV death over 24 months: HR 0.64 (95% CI 0.48-0.85; P=0.002). Confirms COAPT pattern in a second large RCT with moderate-severe functional MR.

evidence.label: Resolution vs MITRA-FR
evidence.source: Anker SD et al. NEJM 2024 (RESHAPE-HF2); Stone Trialist Perspective
evidence.text: RESHAPE-HF2 enrolled patients intermediate between COAPT (disproportionate MR) and MITRA-FR (proportionate MR). Positive result supports procedure benefit across a broader phenotype than COAPT alone.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Key Inclusion Criteria:~* Clinically significant functional mitral regurgitation (moderate-to-severe or severe MR) as defined by European Association of Echocardiography, within 90 days prior to randomization and confirmed by the Echocardiography Core Laboratory Note: The TTE must be obtained after the subject has been stabilized on optimal therapy and has undergone revascularization and/or CRT, as appropriate~* Assessed by the investigator to be on optimal standard of care therapy for heart fa...

title: RapidMeta Cardiology | Transcatheter Mitral for Functional MR v1.0
